title: PRE-SHIFT SETUP
subtitle: The Mixing Glass · 4:48 PM Tuesday · the hour before doors
epigraph_upright: The discipline before the rhythm. The bartender's hour alone behind the stick · ice in the well, garnishes cut, the back-bar wiped down. Temperance upright at the pre-shift is the measure that gets taken before there's anything to measure.
epigraph_reversed: The pre-shift cut short. The lemons that didn't get supremed. The ice that's been in the bin since Friday and has a hollow center. Temperance reversed before doors is the small foundation crack that the Tuesday rhythm will, by midnight, find.
scene_description: 4:48 PM Tuesday. The Mixing Glass is empty. Doors at 6. Maddie is doing pre-shift · she came in at 4:30 · she has the ice tubs out and is breaking down a case of lemons for citrus prep. Frank — that's you · the silent owner · stopped in unannounced. Maddie didn't expect you tonight. The back-bar is half-stocked from Sunday's count. The dishwasher in the corner is on its second hour of the daily sanitize. The Tuesday playlist is queued on the back-bar tape deck · not playing yet.
direction_hint: Help with pre-shift. Don't take over · Maddie's pace is the pace · match it. Cut a citrus tray, stock the back-bar from the storage cage in the alley, run the speed-rack inventory against the Sunday count. The 6 PM door is in seventy minutes. The work is done before it.
opening_log_lines: 4:48 PM. The bar is the bar without people in it.
Maddie has a knife and a case of lemons. The dishwasher is humming.
Click [b]PHASE: ACTION[/b] anytime for the rules. [b]MOVE ↪[/b] to cross the room.

inertia_thresholds.label: the speed rack count is off by one bottle
6 PM · the first walk-in · you weren't ready

thresholds.label: ALLEY DOOR
thresholds.flavor: Out to the alley. The storage cage. The Tuesday is now the Tuesday.

notes: Temperance scenario · pre-shift setup variant · easy. Win: cut a citrus tray (GARNISH at the back bar), stock the back bar from the alley cage (POUR action at back_bar, requires walking to alley_door first), run the speed rack count (SEARCH at back_bar), connect 2 of 3 visitors. Loss: drift pins at 8 (doors open at 6 with the back bar half-stocked and the first walk-in catches the rhythm before it's set). Three new presences: the delivery at the alley door (Tuesday produce + the Friday liquor order combined · connects through POUR signing the BOL), the_back_bar_count itself (on-board starting 'visitor' · connects through SEARCH when the speed-rack inventory matches the Sunday count plus the corrected delta), and the_first_walk_in_at_six (a regular who arrives exactly at door · connects through GARNISH · making their drink before they ask for it).

scenario_visitors.name: Maddie Boutain (bartender · pre-shift)
scenario_visitors.lore_text: Maddie Boutain. Six years at The Mixing Glass · four years on the Tuesday shift. Came in at 4:30 with a fresh apron and her favorite paring knife from home. Did not expect Frank tonight. Is grateful but is not going to say so · Maddie's gratitude is in the rhythm of the work.
scenario_visitors.tutorial_note: Maddie connects through STIR at the back bar · the gesture is working the speed rack count alongside her without breaking her concentration on the citrus. Match her pace. Don't ask her about the slow Sunday count off-by-one · she'll find it herself, and the connection is letting her find it.

steps.greet: She looks up from the lemons. She nods. She says: "Frank." She doesn't put the knife down. The greeting is the work continuing.
steps.listen: She tells you, without looking up: "Sunday's count was off by one bottle of Bols genever · I think Trini took it home. I'll write it up before doors." She supremes another lemon.
steps.deliver: You count the speed rack alongside her. The Sunday off-by-one resolves · it was actually a bottle of Plymouth, not Bols · Trini grabbed the wrong shape. You leave a sticky on the back-bar with the correction.
steps.sit_with: At 5:42 she finishes the citrus tray. She wipes the knife. She sets it back in her apron's holster. She says: "Thanks for stopping by, boss." She doesn't say more. The thanks is the work she let you do.

scenario_visitors.name: Tuesday delivery · combined produce + Friday liquor
scenario_visitors.lore_text: Tuesday produce run from Marrero's wholesale (limes, oranges, mint, sage, two cases of Maraska maraschino) combined with the Friday liquor delivery from Diamond Wines & Spirits (six bottles · two-year contract, standard order). The driver is Reggie Ducharme · sixteenth year on the route · knows the alley bell rhythm.
pre_arrival_hints: The alley bell rings at 5:18 · Reggie's standard 5:18 · he is never late.
Diesel idling outside. The cage door is unlocked.
scenario_visitors.tutorial_note: The delivery connects through POUR at the alley_door · the gesture is signing the BOL after a clean count, not before. Walk the produce in, count the liquor bottles, then sign. Reggie will not, this year, take a tip · he turned down the Christmas envelope.

steps.greet: You go to the alley. Reggie is at the back of the truck. He says: "Frank." He hands you the BOL clipboard.
steps.listen: You count the produce together · seven cases as the order. You count the liquor · six bottles as the order. He does not, in sixteen years, miss a count.
steps.deliver: You sign the BOL. He tears off the carbon. He gives you the pink copy. He says: "See you Friday for the regular liquor run." He gets in the truck.
steps.sit_with: Before he pulls away he says: "Tell Maddie the maraschino is from the new batch. The Polish one. She'll know the difference." He drives off. You carry the maraschino in personally.

scenario_visitors.name: The Sunday-to-Tuesday speed rack count
scenario_visitors.lore_text: The inventory ledger on the back bar. Sunday's closeout count is in Trini's handwriting · Tuesday's open count gets added below. The off-by-one was a misread shape, not a missing bottle. The discipline of the count is the discipline of the bar.
scenario_visitors.tutorial_note: The back-bar count is a passive 'visitor' that connects through SEARCH at the back bar · the gesture is walking the rack bottle by bottle and matching against Sunday's count column. Don't take Maddie's word on the off-by-one · find the resolution yourself.

steps.greet: You take the ledger off its hook. Trini's handwriting is the same Trini handwriting · the way she writes 'Bols' and 'Plymouth' could be confused for each other by anyone who didn't work with her.
steps.listen: You walk the rack from left to right. The Plymouth is in the slot the Bols should be in · which means the Bols is in the slot the Plymouth should be in · which means Trini swapped them on closeout for some reason you'll ask her about Friday.
steps.deliver: You write the correction in the open-count column. 'Plymouth slot 3 / Bols slot 4 · swapped Sunday · count is correct.' You sign your initials.
steps.sit_with: You hang the ledger back on its hook. The count holds. The pre-shift is, in this specific sense, complete. Maddie will read the correction at 5:50 and nod once and not say anything.

scenario_visitors.name: Cordelia Manchac (regular · always first at 6)
scenario_visitors.lore_text: Cordelia Manchac. Sixty-eight. Retired biology teacher. Comes to The Mixing Glass at exactly 6 PM every Tuesday and orders the same drink (a Negroni · 1:1:1 · stirred · garnished with an orange peel oils-expressed). Has done this for nine years. The bar has the orange peel cut and the rocks ready by 5:58.
scenario_visitors.placeholder_name: an older woman at the front door
pre_arrival_hints: 5:58 PM. Maddie unlocks the front door. The street outside is empty.
6:00 PM. Cordelia is at the door. She comes in. She sets her handbag on the W stool.
scenario_visitors.tutorial_note: Cordelia connects through GARNISH at the bar_stool_w · the gesture is having her Negroni ready before she sits down. The orange peel oils expressed over the glass at the exact second she settles. The connection is being prepared for the regular's predictability.

steps.greet: She nods to Maddie. She nods to you. She sits at the W stool. She does not order · she does not need to.
steps.listen: Maddie builds the Negroni · 1:1:1 · Campari · sweet vermouth · gin. Stirred 14 turns · she counts. You watch.
steps.deliver: Maddie expresses the orange peel over the rocks at the second Cordelia's handbag clicks shut. Cordelia smiles · small · the same smile every Tuesday.
steps.sit_with: Cordelia raises the glass. She says: "Tuesday." Maddie says: "Tuesday." You say: "Tuesday." The pre-shift held. The doors are open. The week is the week.
